title: Emily Spanish assignment — deforestation/wildfires essay + Rankett demo flow stitched in
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir helps Emily write an Oregon environmental-issues essay (English draft → Spanish translation): two problems — deforestation and wildfires — with personal stake ("I was born and raised in Oregon").
  • Bilingual structure: each Spanish paragraph follows the English original word-for-word with parenthetical English back-translations — built so Emily can present it in her university Spanish course without owning the Spanish syntax herself.
  • The doc is aggressively two-headed: tabs 4–5 abruptly pivot from the Spanish essay into a full Rankett demo-flow walkthrough ("3 Weeks, 121 Commits, 100% ghost white label AI visibility platform with three portals, dual auth, Stripe billing, Kanban workflow").
  • The Rankett demo doc inside Emily's homework doc is a perfect snapshot of founder context collapse — the same Google Doc tab is the only surface his attention is on.
  • Soft-mush opener at the top of the Spanish section: "Bby, i love you soooooo much so much so much. xoxxxoxoxox" — the doc is part-pillow-talk, part-coursework, part-pitch-deck.

The Spanish work

The essay argues two problems that recur across Oregon policy:

  1. Deforestation — driven by housing/infrastructure demand, destructive to native species, framed as a moral violation of stewardship.
  2. Wildfires — natural-and-man-made, worsened by underfunded forest-floor maintenance, displacing residents.

Recommendations: increase land-management funding (public + private), stricter logging laws, personal volunteering with replanting orgs, dry-season vigilance about campfires/vehicle sparks. The framing carries the "impoverished communities first, damage irreversible" equity beat — same instinct Emily brings to her FHS food insecurity brief three weeks later.

The Rankett demo flow (Tab 5)

Same doc abruptly contains the full agent-readable demo script for app.rankett.com:

  • Agency sign-up flow — Google OAuth + email, webhook auto-creates agency record in Supabase, redirects to /agency/setup.
  • Agency dashboard — stats cards (clients, avg visibility score, analyses-this-month, subscription tier), portfolio trend chart, activity feed.
  • Add a client — simple form (company / website / email), creates client with JWT credentials, white-labeled portal.
  • Run AI analysis — streaming progress through 4 stages, multi-AI parallel queries (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity), produces 0–100 visibility score + A–F grade + breakdown by mention rate / citation quality / position / competitive presence.

Why both live in one doc

The implicit answer is that this Google Doc is Yahir's working surface during the Feb 2–3 push — Emily's homework and the Rankett demo dry-run are both happening between the same two tabs. The vault's broader Emily arc gets a small data-point: helping with school is a love-language Yahir uses inside the same window where his attention is on shipping product.

Privacy notes

  • Emily's name not in this doc directly (already a real entity).
  • No credentials in the demo flow — JWT/token mentions are generic feature descriptions, not real keys.

Connections
